design_philosophy.core_loop: Deploy specialized squad units across isometric battlefields, manage unit health via medic resources, capture enemy capital through tactical positioning and real-time combat execution
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balance between strategic unit composition/positioning and real-time action execution; resource scarcity of medic revivals creates consequence for tactical mistakes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a specialized military squad with diverse roles (gunner, launcher, chucker, scorcher, blaster) in tactical warfare against AI or human opponents; cartoon satirization of military conflict
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid action-strategy design that refuses pure turn-based tactics or pure action—forces simultaneous strategic and execution skill. Medic revival system creates permanent consequences without permadeath. Satirical tone (brothers Chaos/Havoc, fictional countries Moronica/Viceria, Chuck Norris reference) commits to comedic framing of warfare

difficulty.lives_system: No traditional lives; soldiers can be revived by medics up to a resource-limited number of times per battle
difficulty.continues_system: Campaign mode presents series of battles across dynamic strategic map; multiplayer supports four simultaneous players
difficulty.checkpointing: Battle-based progression; victory completes one battle segment and advances campaign map
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Four squad class options available; players select team composition before battle, affecting strategic flexibility and difficulty approach. Commando option offers greater manual control at cost of reduced unit count (2 vs 5 soldiers)

progression.upgrade_system: Point-based system: breaking point thresholds awards additional medic revives; collected battlefield items yield points
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign mode progresses through battles fought on dynamic strategic map toward capturing enemy capital. Tutorial 'boot camp' mode available for learning
replayability_hooks: Four distinct team compositions with different tactical profiles
Up to four-player simultaneous multiplayer (cooperative vs computer or competitive player-vs-player)
Dynamic strategic map creates variable battle sequences across campaign playthroughs
Multiple class specializations reward mastery of different playstyles

world_building_techniques: Character backstory (brothers Chaos/Havoc, childhood rivalry over comic book escalating to military dictatorship)
Fictional nation names (Moronica, Viceria) establishing satirical tone
Visual class design (Chuck Norris headband reference on Chucker, character silhouettes defining roles)
Gameplay framing as 'quick and dirty' battles or prolonged campaign conquest

faction_indicators: General Chaos faction (player-selectable blue/red team affiliation)
danger_signals: Scorcher units: flamethrower-equipped soldiers capable of multi-target damage and difficult-to-escape close-range engagements
Launcher units: instant-death instant-kill potential (victim reduced to skeleton, unrevivable)
Blaster units: instant-death capability with dynamite attacks
Low medic revival resource limits creating vulnerability to attrition
environmental_storytelling: Isometric battlefield layout with destructible objective targets (rockets/explosives can destroy)
Water terrain affecting Scorcher unit functionality (temporarily disables flamethrower)
Cartoon visual aesthetic contextualizing satirical military conflict narrative

sources_used: Wikipedia: General Chaos (gameplay mechanics, plot, classes, reception)
